summary:
Accepted: Q046-Q048 use S_KOGAS_ERP and Q049-Q052 use S_BONGHWA_DISASTER while Q053 returns to S_KOGAS_ERP, matching manifest preferred order and the one selected technical seed policy; both primary and secondary variants use seed_policy "one_fixed_seed_per_run_no_cross_seed_synthesis". Accepted: G_TECHNICAL_REQUIREMENT_FIELDS and G_SELECTED_PROJECT_MEMORY are active for all primary/secondary technical cases, and ADD is consistently used for all *_technical_add_transfer variants as a sparse-field probe.
Risky: ADD proxy variants still mirror full technical question forms, so downstream scoring pipelines must explicitly treat "variant_claim": "secondary sparse-technical seed to test not-found behavior" as diagnostic and exclude these from main evidence. Risk remains if not-found handling is weak because secondary variants can return false positives on sparse fields.
Needs review: confirm evaluation harness explicitly enforces no cross-seed fusion and explicit not-found handling on sparse outputs, because Q046-Q053 technical topics can include genuinely missing fields.
proposal: Keep Q046-Q053 primary cases as evidence path and route *_technical_add_transfer to diagnostic-only scoring with variant_claim filtering plus strict not-found checks.
validation: Verified Q046-Q053 in selected_project_batch_l121.csv: Q046/Q047/Q048 use S_KOGAS_ERP, Q049-Q052 use S_BONGHWA_DISASTER, Q053 uses S_KOGAS_ERP.
Verified manifest rows Q046-Q053 require one selected technical seed per run, no cross-seed synthesis, and not-found for sparse fields.

Verified technical secondary variants l121_Q046_technical_add_transfer through l121_Q053_technical_add_transfer each use single seed_refs ["S_ADD_LARGE_TRANSFER"] and variant_claim "secondary sparse-technical seed to test not-found behavior".
